meaning: n.(对毁坏,损坏的)赔偿,赔礼
examples: He wanted to make amends for causing their marriage to fail.
She was sincere and genuine in her wish to make amends for the past.
He was full of remorse and asked Beatrice what he could do to make amends.
Hoolahan almost made amends shortly afterwards but his 12-yard strike was tipped around the post by Turner.
His only bogey came at the 10th but he made amends with an eagle at the par-five 17th.
